beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and light breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Asa wakes early with full energy on a sunny Monday; meticulous planner and fitness-focused, so morning routine is quick and efficient.

beats.activity: Trail run at Marymoor Park before work
beats.reasoning: Sunny, 74F high—perfect trail-running weather; Marymoor is near Redmond workplace and fits obsession with fitness; energizes the day.

beats.activity: Commute from Marymoor Park to first hospice patient visit in Redmond
beats.reasoning: Short buffer to freshen up and drive to first home-visit patient in Redmond; hospice nurse schedule typically starts 8:30am or earlier.
Hospice nursing involves multi-hour home visits; meticulous planner schedules around patient needs; avoids the FIFA match at 11am by design.

beats.activity: Drive to second hospice patient visit in north Seattle
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure (Westlake–U District, 9am–3pm) doesn't affect Redmond-to-Shoreline drive; generous 30-min buffer for across-town movement.

beats.activity: Second hospice patient home visit in Shoreline
beats.reasoning: Typical 2-hour hospice visit; Asa is a meticulous professional and won't interrupt for 11am FIFA match—work takes priority.

beats.activity: Lunch break at a nearby café
beats.reasoning: Asa stops for a quick, healthy lunch between home visits; sunny weather makes outdoor seating appealing; stays in Shoreline to minimize travel.

beats.activity: Drive to third hospice patient visit in Ballard
beats.reasoning: Across-town drive (Shoreline to Ballard); Light Rail closure still in effect but doesn't affect car travel; 40-min buffer for traffic and setup.

beats.activity: Third hospice patient home visit in Ballard
beats.reasoning: Final home visit of the day; meticulous schedule allows Asa to end work day by mid-afternoon and have leisure time.
Ballard to Bellevue is a 40+ min drive across Lake Washington; Asa plans meticulous routes and values getting home before evening.

beats.activity: Chess study and online match with a close friend
beats.reasoning: Asa values time with close friends over crowds; plays chess regularly; perfect way to unwind after a full day of emotionally demanding hospice work.
Healthy, home-cooked meal fits Asa's fitness obsession and meticulous self-care routine; relaxed evening at home.

diary: Started the day right with a trail run at Marymoor—the park was perfect this morning, quiet and cool before the heat set in. Three patient visits today kept me moving between Redmond, Shoreline, and Ballard. The first patient in Redmond was restless; we adjusted her comfort meds and talked through some family things. The second visit in Shoreline felt heavier somehow—I sat with him for a while after the clinical work was done. Grabbed lunch at a café nearby, just needed a moment to breathe. The Ballard visit was gentler, more peaceful. Drove home as the evening cooled down. Played chess online with an old friend later—we've been playing for years, and tonight I needed that. Made pasta, ate slowly. There's a particular exhaustion that comes with this work, but also something that settles you.
thought_process: The light rail closure didn't affect me much since I was driving all day anyway. The World Cup buzz downtown felt distant—bars opening early for the match, but I was already out on the road by then. Running this morning helped; I needed that clarity before stepping into three homes where people are preparing to die. Each visit required a different energy, different presence. The second patient's heaviness stuck with me through lunch. Chess tonight was deliberate—I needed something tactile and strategic, something that engages the mind differently than caregiving does. The weather being beautiful felt almost ironic given the day's weight, but I'm grateful for it anyway.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
